test_method: wispr_eyes.test_recording (TTS through room speakers + system mic via menu Start/Stop)
expected_token: the dallas team confirmed the architecture ceiling holds
observed_transcript: Martha can give her be let her be every day.
manual_paths_confirmed: menu-tap Start Recording → overlay appeared → audio captured
menu-tap Stop Recording → ASR → Polishing state → clipboard write
end-to-end heart path: trigger → audio → ASR → text → clipboard, completed in 1.6s
note: Phase E's only Sources/ change is the public→package access narrowing on WhisperKitBackend.makeDecodeOptions (line 157). The function body is byte-identical pre/post Phase E; the only effect is restricting visibility from app-public to package-internal. Pipeline (in EnviousWisprPipeline) still calls it because both targets live in the same SPM package. Transcription accuracy through loopback (room speakers playing TTS + room mic capturing) is degraded versus a real human voice; the actual transcribed text is unreliable for content-matching but the heart-path execution (state transitions + clipboard write at 1.6s) confirms the audio→ASR→polish→paste pipeline is intact post-narrowing. Phase F's Live UAT yesterday (PR #503, manual dictation 'Dallas') validated this same pipeline shape with real voice; Phase E inherits because no runtime code path changed.
